parsed_review.summary: CP00-431 is a descriptor-only settlement-core (RP14) closeout pack: 40 units, risk class B, range RP14.P02.M04.S13-RP14.P02.M06.S08 (M04 Secondary Workflow Slice tail 10 rows, M05 Permission And Audit Binding 22 rows, M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head 8 rows), following the established CP426-CP430 pattern. The pack does NOT claim production/runtime readiness (manifest and command-evidence both report production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, status implemented_pending_review). Its only positive claim is the descriptor-verification flag (settlement_core_p02_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor_verified), which is well supported: (1) implementation is pure/frozen — service.js only imports registry.js and returns Object.freeze structures with no fs/db/net/process I/O; (2) deterministic coverage and slice validators check unit counts, deliverable/phase/micro distributions, required section rows, descriptor_only/runtime_execution:false guards, and authority boundaries; (3) model.test.js carries top-level asserts plus three CP431 tests covering coverage (40 units; M04=10, M05=22, M06=8), descriptor-only rows, evidence packets, and handoff (CP00-431-to-CP00-432, next RP14.P02.M06.S09); (4) the contract is kept in sync via deep-equal checks of current_pack, no_write_attestation, cp431_requirements, and p02_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor, with historical_pack_bindings ending at CP00-431 and prior descriptors preserved (no regression). command-evidence.json records all repo-wide validate commands, node --test, npm test, npm run validate/build, and git diff --check at exit 0. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are extensively attested (no_real_data:true, writes_product_state:false, all dispatch/persist/audit/storage flags false) and confirmed by inspection. Review hardening (CP178+) preconditions are clean: readiness-report auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1439-byte valid JSON result — not 0-byte, malformed, fenced, or tool-call-shaped), validation-report confirms prompt within size limits, no forbidden tools, schema present, and summary-only prohibited (Risk B + security/audit + review-evidence deliverables) so full source inspection was required and performed; forbidden_review_evidence enumerates all reject conditions. Claude authority boundary is preserved everywhere (claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false, claude_not_final_approver:true; human final approval still required). Hermes H14 patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor evidence packet/gate validation (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, hermes_packet_body_included:false; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product:false). Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly absent and declared pending (pack_level_claude_review.status:pending, valid_review_receipts:[]), which is correct for this review stage. No P0/P1/P2 issues found. One non-blocking P3 documentation/bookkeeping nuance in the changed-file-scope artifact. Closeout is not blocked by this review; the enterprise trust / production claim remains open for human final approval as designed.

findings.title: changed-file-scope labels the pack's own cp00-431 evidence directory as unrelated_dirty_files_preserved
findings.details: changed-file-scope.json lists docs/closeout-packs/cp00-431/ (the pack's own manifest/command-evidence directory) and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 137-138), while simultaneously listing them in untracked_implementation_files (lines 130-133). This is also inconsistent with the manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest lines 1720-1728), which correctly excludes the pack's own cp00-431 directory and treats it as related. The dual/loose 'unrelated' classification of a pack-owned artifact directory could mislead a future auditor about what is in scope. It does not affect scope integrity: active_pack_implementation_refs explicitly enumerate the eight tracked implementation files, and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, so nothing in scope was omitted or misattributed for the review.
findings.recommendation: Tighten the changed-file-scope generator so a pack's own docs/closeout-packs/<pack>/ evidence directory is not labeled under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, aligning its semantics with the manifest's equivalent list. Non-blocking; no action required for CP00-431 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational, non-blocking. Recorded for evidence-artifact consistency; does not affect pack scope integrity, no-write/no-real-data boundaries, or the production_ready determination, and does not block pack or goal closeout.
